metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the target runbook (`infra/runbooks/two-vote-gate-rollback.md`) explicitly to the plan's back-out section, directing S6 to EXTEND that file with the ACL-revoke + submit-type-revert + bot-rollback procedures rather than creating a separate document.
findings.finding: The S6 plan says 'back-out documented + TESTED on the live Gerrit' but does not name `infra/runbooks/two-vote-gate-rollback.md` as the target to extend. The epic's last plan-re-verification comment explicitly directs 'S6 back-out should EXTEND two-vote-gate-rollback.md (not duplicate)' — but this instruction is absent from the plan text. Because `two-vote-gate-rollback.md` already owns the gate-config back-out surface (§A/§B), an S6 implementer following only the plan text could create a separate, duplicate back-out section rather than extending the existing runbook, fragmenting the operator control surface.
findings.location: What section / AC line: 'Back-out documented + TESTED on the live Gerrit'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What/AC back-out item, replace 'Back-out documented + TESTED on the live Gerrit' with 'Back-out documented + TESTED on the live Gerrit: extend `infra/runbooks/two-vote-gate-rollback.md` with a §D (feature-branch flow back-out) covering ACL revoke, submit-type revert, and bot rollback — do NOT create a separate runbook.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/runbooks/two-vote-gate-rollback.md` to the plan's edit targets (What + AC3 + file_impact): the feature-branch ACL revoke, submit-type pin revert, and bot-image rollback back-out steps must extend that runbook per the post-1fa8 note, not a parallel document.
findings.finding: infra/runbooks/two-vote-gate-rollback.md is the acknowledged home for the feature-branch back-out procedure (per the post-1fa8 ticket comment) but is absent from the plan's 'What' section, AC3, and the story's file_impact — making it an unenumerated edit target for the back-out work.
findings.location: What / Back-out section; AC3; story file_impact
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's 'What' back-out bullet and AC3, replace the generic 'Back-out documented' with 'Back-out section added to infra/runbooks/two-vote-gate-rollback.md'; add that file to file_impact.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the inactivity detection criterion for the stale-branch inventory script (e.g., 'no patchsets pushed to the branch in > N days, determined via `GET /projects/rebar/branches/feature%2F*/reflog` or by listing open changes targeting the branch via `GET /changes/?q=project:rebar+branch:feature/<name>+status:open`'), and distinguish the merged-back detection path (branch tip is an ancestor of main) from the abandoned-but-open-changes path.
findings.finding: The stale-branch inventory script cannot be correctly implemented without a specified definition of 'inactive': the plan says the script 'flags feature/* branches inactive beyond the cap' but does not define what Gerrit-observable attribute signals inactivity (e.g., last patchset timestamp on open changes targeting the branch, the branch's own last-push timestamp via `GET /projects/{}/branches/{}/reflog`, or absence of any open changes targeting the branch). Different definitions produce different false-positive/false-negative rates — a branch with a very recent push but all changes merged could be 'active' by push-time but 'done' by open-change count, or vice-versa. Without specifying the detection criterion, the implementer must invent it and may produce a script that silently misses stale branches.
findings.location: What — 'Stale-branch inventory' bullet; AC — 'Stale-branch inventory script committed + runbook entry (flag + owner-confirmed delete; covers abandoned branches)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a GitHub mirror branch cleanup step to the stale-branch inventory script and runbook entry: after owner-confirmed Gerrit deletion, also delete the corresponding `refs/heads/feature/<name>` from the GitHub mirror (e.g. via `gh api -X DELETE repos/{GITHUB_REPO}/git/refs/heads/feature/<name>`) — as referenced by the explicit callout in `replication.config` line 76.
findings.finding: The stale-branch inventory script and runbook entry do not address GitHub mirror branch cleanup: `replication.config` explicitly sets `mirror = false` on the `feature/*` push refspec (confirmed in the live codebase), so deleting a `feature/*` branch in Gerrit does NOT auto-prune it from the GitHub mirror. The plan's stale-branch script only 'flags feature/* branches inactive beyond the cap for owner-confirmed deletion' — with no mention of a corresponding GitHub-side branch delete step. Without this, abandoned or merged-back feature branches accumulate indefinitely on the GitHub mirror, which undermines the stated goal of lifecycle hygiene.
findings.location: What / Scope — 'Stale-branch inventory' bullet; also infra/gerrit/replication.config line 76 (the explicit callout: 'deletion in Gerrit does NOT auto-prune them on GitHub — the S6 stale-branch inventory + the merge-back cleanup handle GitHub-side feature-branch teardown')
findings.suggested_fix: In the stale-branch inventory script (and the merge-back procedure in CONTRIBUTING.md §4e), add a final step: `gh api -X DELETE "repos/${GITHUB_REPO}/git/refs/heads/feature/<name>"` (mirroring the pattern already used in `reviewbot-e2e.sh`'s cleanup trap). Document in the runbook that GitHub cleanup is the second leg of feature-branch deletion.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving sequence to AC3: e.g., 'after ACL revoke, push a test merge commit to a sandbox repo and confirm Gerrit returns a permission-denied error; capture the response as evidence'.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Back-out tested live: ACL revoke restores single-change flow') names an observable outcome but provides no proving command — there is no specified test sequence (e.g., 'attempt to push a merge commit after ACL revocation and confirm Gerrit rejects it with the expected error') that would produce durable evidence of success.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC3: 'Back-out tested live: ACL revoke restores single-change flow'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Ensure the branch-lifetime cap value is externalised (e.g., as a named variable at the top of the script or in a shared config/env file) so it can be changed without editing implementation logic.
findings.finding: The branch-lifetime cap (the core threshold of the stale-branch inventory script) is described as a policy value but the plan does not specify whether it will be externalised as a configurable parameter (e.g., a variable at the top of the script or in a config file) rather than hardcoded inside the script body.
findings.location: Stale-branch inventory / 'Lifetime cap + cadence policy written' AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single `BRANCH_LIFETIME_DAYS` variable (or equivalent) at the top of the inventory script (or read from a repo-level config), and reference that variable in the AC so reviewers can confirm it is not hardcoded inline.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit to a single, specific path for the stale-branch inventory script (e.g. `infra/scripts/stale-branch-inventory.sh` or `infra/gerrit/stale-branch-inventory.sh`) in the plan's What, AC2, and file_impact — resolve the 'infra/scripts or infra/gerrit' ambiguity.
findings.finding: The stale-branch inventory script target path is specified only as 'infra/scripts or infra/gerrit' — an ambiguous disjunction that prevents AC2 verification and creates a mismatch risk with the path already referenced in infra/gerrit/replication.config.
findings.location: What / Stale-branch inventory; AC2; story file_impact lists only 'infra/scripts/observability.sh' as the potential co-location
findings.suggested_fix: Pick `infra/scripts/stale-branch-inventory.sh` (alongside other operator scripts) and name it explicitly throughout; update replication.config comment to reference the chosen path once decided.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify error-handling behavior for the inventory script: non-zero exit on Gerrit API failure, a log/alert on partial results, and at least one retry before failing, so operators are notified rather than seeing a false-clean report.
findings.finding: The stale-branch inventory script calls the Gerrit API (an external dependency) with no error handling, retry, or observability specified — a silent failure would cause the script to report a false-clean inventory, leaving stale branches undetected.
findings.location: What / Stale-branch inventory script (infra/scripts or infra/gerrit)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the runbook entry: script must exit non-zero and emit a named log line (e.g. `INVENTORY_FAILED`) on any Gerrit API error; wire the scheduled invocation to a monitoring alert on non-zero exit; document a single retry with backoff before failure.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete branch lifetime cap (e.g., 90 days of inactivity) and merge-cadence interval (e.g., bi-weekly) directly in the plan so the script threshold and policy text are unambiguous.
findings.finding: The branch lifetime cap and catch-up merge cadence values are never specified — the executor must invent them. The stale-branch script cannot be written, and the policy cannot be written, without a concrete threshold (e.g., 90 days inactive) and a cadence (e.g., weekly sync). These are real design decisions, not conventions inferrable from the epic.
findings.location: What bullet 1: 'Branch-lifetime cap + catch-up merge cadence documented'; AC1: 'Lifetime cap + cadence policy written'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Lifetime cap = 90 days of no new commits or review activity; catch-up merge cadence = every two weeks (Fridays). Script flags any feature/* branch with last-commit date > 90 days and no open review.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the runbook: the confirmation mechanism (how owners are notified and confirm), a timeout after which an unresponded flag escalates to a team lead or is treated as abandoned, and a dispute/override path for contested deletions.
findings.finding: The owner-confirmation step for stale-branch deletion ('owner-confirmed delete') has no defined non-happy-path: the plan does not specify the confirmation mechanism (Gerrit comment, email, CLI prompt?), what happens if the owner does not respond within the lifetime cap period, or how a contested deletion (owner disputes the stale classification) is escalated.
findings.location: AC: 'Stale-branch inventory script committed + runbook entry (flag + owner-confirmed delete; covers abandoned branches)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify where stale-branch flags are recorded (e.g., ephemeral stdout only vs. a durable store), define who owns and retires that record, and confirm the script is idempotent under concurrent or repeated runs.
findings.finding: The stale-branch inventory script produces 'flags' for branches exceeding the lifetime cap, but the plan does not specify where those flags are recorded (stdout, a file, a Gerrit label, a ticket system). If flags are written to a persistent store, there is no described retirement path for that flag state — flags for already-deleted or already-merged branches may accumulate indefinitely, and concurrent operator runs could double-flag or double-delete.
findings.location: Stale-branch inventory (What section + AC: 'Stale-branch inventory script committed + runbook entry')
findings.suggested_fix: If flags are ephemeral (script prints to stdout and operator acts immediately), state that explicitly to close the retirement question. If a durable list is maintained (e.g., a YAML file in the repo), add a 'prune confirmed-deleted entries' step to the runbook and note that the script should skip already-deleted refs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name `docs/adr/0025-feature-branch-merge-carry.md` explicitly in the What section and file_impact as the file receiving the new 'Cost & latency' section (AC4).
findings.finding: docs/adr/0025-feature-branch-merge-carry.md (the 'amending ADR') is the target for the new 'Cost & latency' section, but it is absent from the plan's file_impact and not named explicitly in the What/AC4 text — the implementer must infer it from the phrase 'the amending ADR'.
findings.location: What / Cost & latency section; AC4; story file_impact
findings.suggested_fix: In the What bullet and AC4, replace 'the amending ADR' with 'docs/adr/0025-feature-branch-merge-carry.md'; add that path to file_impact.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the copyCondition disposition in the plan: either 'leave it (no action required, state this explicitly in the runbook)' or 'revert it (add a step and a corresponding AC check)'.
findings.finding: The plan defers the copyCondition back-out decision to the executor ('may be left or reverted') without providing a criterion for which choice to make or who decides, making the back-out procedure incomplete as a specification.
findings.location: What bullet 3: 'the copyCondition addition is inert absent merge changes and may be left or reverted'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether AC4 requires all three metrics unconditionally, or explicitly state the fallback (e.g., 'if LLM cost is unavailable from the runner, document that it is not instrumented and record N/A with rationale').
findings.finding: The third Cost & latency metric ('per-review LLM cost if surfaced by the runner') is conditional on a runtime circumstance outside the plan's control, making AC4 partially non-evaluable: it is unclear whether AC4 is complete with two metrics or requires all three.
findings.location: AC4: 'ADR Cost & latency section populated with the three named metrics from S5 data'; What bullet 4: 'per-review LLM cost if surfaced by the runner'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'per-review LLM cost if surfaced by the runner' to 'per-review LLM cost (record N/A with an explanation if the runner does not expose this)' so AC4 is always completable and auditable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC 3) requiring that the copyCondition disposition (left in place or reverted) is explicitly recorded in the runbook or ADR, so the as-built state is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The What section describes a decision about the copyCondition addition ('may be left or reverted') but no acceptance criterion covers this choice or its outcome, leaving an uncovered described deliverable/decision.
findings.location: What section, bullet 3 ('copyCondition addition is inert absent merge changes and may be left or reverted') vs AC 3 ('Back-out tested live: ACL revoke restores single-change flow; submit-type revert verified…')
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC 3 with: 'copyCondition disposition (retained or reverted) recorded in the runbook with rationale.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific ADR file path (e.g., `docs/adr/0042-feature-branch-flow.md`) in the plan so reviewers can directly locate the document to verify the cost/latency section.
findings.finding: The 'amending ADR' is referenced throughout the plan but its file path and title are never named, making it ambiguous which document owns the cost/latency section and whether reviewers can locate it to confirm the section lands in the right place.
findings.location: What section / 'Cost & latency section in the amending ADR' AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the back-out steps as an ordered, verify-each-step checklist in the runbook, with explicit instructions for recovering from a partial failure at each step (e.g., 'if step 2 fails, steps already completed are safe to leave while re-attempting step 2').
findings.finding: The back-out sequence (ACL revoke + submit-type revert) involves multiple discrete write operations on the live Gerrit but has no partial-failure handling or idempotency guarantee — if the sequence is interrupted mid-way (e.g., pushMerge revoked but feature/* ACL still in place), the system is left in an inconsistent mixed state with no recovery path specified.
findings.location: What / Back-out documented + TESTED; AC: 'Back-out tested live: ACL revoke restores single-change flow'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the bot rollback runbook entry: specify whether in-flight reviews must be drained or manually resolved before the image swap, and what operator action is needed for open changes that were awaiting new-bot labels at rollback time.
findings.finding: The bot rollback path ('redeploy the prior review-bot image') does not specify the state of in-flight reviews or feature-branch merges that are mid-process when the rollback occurs — the old bot image may mis-handle events queued by the new bot, or in-flight merges may stall waiting for a label the restored bot no longer posts.
findings.location: What / 'bot code back-out = redeploy the prior review-bot image'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `CONTRIBUTING.md` to the story's file_impact and explicitly note in the What section that the lifetime cap + catch-up cadence policy requires a cross-reference addition to the existing CONTRIBUTING.md §4 feature-branch section.
findings.finding: CONTRIBUTING.md is an unenumerated edit target for AC1: the lifetime cap + cadence policy must be 'referenced from CONTRIBUTING/runbook', but CONTRIBUTING.md is absent from the story's file_impact.
findings.location: AC1: 'Lifetime cap + cadence policy written and referenced from CONTRIBUTING/runbook'; story file_impact

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
